tests.name: NOT YET signal appears after first qualifying session shows NOT YET with streak 1 of 2 and the RR criterion after the first qualifying session
NOT YET signal appears after first qualifying session criterion summary line is visible in the signal card without tapping anything
READY signal appears when the advancement criterion is fully met shows READY TO ADVANCE and the next exercise after 2 consecutive qualifying sessions
REVIEW signal explains form variance without judging the practitioner shows REVIEW RECOMMENDED when form quality range across last 3 qualifying sessions is 2 or more
Full rationale accordion shows session evidence with RR wiki attribution rationale data includes session evidence table entries and wiki citation
Advancing to the next exercise records the progression with cited evidence records a progression event citing the qualifying sessions when user advances
Advancing to the next exercise records the progression with cited evidence reverts the advancement when user taps undo within 5 seconds
First session for an exercise shows an instructional message, not a signal state returns null signal when user has never logged this exercise before
Paywall preview appears after 3 sessions for free-tier users free-tier user with exactly 3 sessions sees upgrade prompt alongside the signal
Error: readiness signal when history is insufficient streak resets to zero when a non-qualifying session interrupts a consecutive run
Error: readiness signal when history is insufficient streak resets to zero when more than 14 days pass between qualifying sessions
Error: readiness signal when history is insufficient signal cannot be computed when exercise_id is not in the RR registry
Error: readiness signal when history is insufficient advancement is rejected when qualifying session IDs are empty
Error: readiness signal when history is insufficient REVIEW signal copy is informative and not punitive
Exercise autocomplete returns RR suggestions quickly returns Pike Push-up as the first result when searching 'pike'
Exercise autocomplete returns RR suggestions quickly resolves 'pike pushup' and 'PPP progression' to the same exercise as 'Pike Push-up'
Exercise autocomplete returns RR suggestions quickly returns no results and allows free-text when exercise is not in the RR library
Logging a session stores the training data correctly stores session with RR exercise ID when exercise is from the registry
Logging a session stores the training data correctly saves a session without form quality when user skips that field
Logging a session stores the training data correctly saves a session with a free-text exercise name when exercise is not in the registry
Logging multiple exercises in a single training session stores all exercises as entries in the same session
Logging multiple exercises in a single training session allows multiple entries for the same exercise within one session
Closed session cannot have new entries added (session state machine) rejects adding an entry to a closed session
Session saved and queued when device is offline stores session in the local offline queue when there is no connectivity
Session saved and queued when device is offline syncs offline sessions automatically when device reconnects
Error: exercise not found in the RR registry returns an empty results list when the exercise name does not match any RR exercise
Error: offline sync conflict resolved by last-write-wins newer session timestamp wins when two sessions have the same user, exercise, and date key
Rep trend is visible for the last 6 sessions of an exercise returns the last 6 sessions for an exercise in chronological order
Rep trend is visible for the last 6 sessions of an exercise returns all sessions when fewer than 6 have been logged
Form quality trend is visible alongside the rep trend returns sessions that show both reps and form quality for each entry
Progress history is accessible from the readiness card and from the session list findByUserAndExercise returns all sessions for the exercise regardless of entry point
Error: history view returns an empty list for a new exercise with no sessions returns an empty list when the user has never logged the exercise
Error: free-tier history is limited to the last 30 days sessions older than 30 days are not returned for a free-tier user
Error: flat trend visible in history data for plateau detection precondition 5 sessions with identical rep counts show a flat trend in the returned data
Push progression chain shows all RR push exercises in order returns all RR push exercises in chain order with attribution links
Push progression chain shows all RR push exercises in order each exercise in the push chain carries its RR advancement criteria
User can identify their current position in the push chain current exercise is findable by ID in the push chain when user has progressed
Tapping an exercise shows its RR criteria and wiki citation findById returns the full exercise data including criteria and wiki URL
Pull progression chain shows RR pull exercises in order (Release 2) returns all RR pull exercises in chain order when pull track is requested
Error: exercise not found when requesting details for an unknown ID findById returns null for an exercise ID that does not exist in the registry
Error: progression tree shows no current position for a user with no progression state getCurrentProgression returns null for a new user who has not yet set a current exercise
Error: progression history is empty for a user who has never advanced findHistory returns an empty list for a user who has never advanced exercises
Error: advancement rejected when qualifying session IDs are not provided advance command rejects when no qualifying sessions are cited (DM3 invariant)
Plateau warning fires after 3 consecutive sessions with no rep increase detects a plateau and returns a warning with the flat rep trend
Plateau warning fires after 3 consecutive sessions with no rep increase detects a plateau when 4 consecutive sessions have identical rep counts
Plateau warning fires after 3 consecutive sessions with no rep increase detects a plateau when rep count declines across 3 sessions
No plateau warning fires when reps are increasing normally returns null when rep counts are increasing across consecutive sessions
No plateau warning fires when reps are increasing normally returns null when fewer than 3 sessions have been logged for the exercise
Plateau warning card shows raw trend data with dates and an actionable suggestion warning includes the rep count for each trailing session and an RR wiki deload link
Plateau detection is stateless and recomputed from history each time detector returns a warning every time it is called with flat session data, regardless of prior dismiss actions
Error: plateau detection handles empty session history gracefully returns null when no sessions have been logged for the exercise
Error: a single rep increase breaks a plateau streak and resets the count does not fire warning when the most recent session shows a rep increase after a flat run

Marco makes his first progression decision (online) logs a push session with sets, reps, and form quality

Marco makes his first progression decision (online) can see their current position in the push progression chain
Sofia logs a session when her device has no connectivity session is stored locally when the device is offline and syncs automatically on reconnect

SessionRepository (via SessionPort) addEntry() appends an exercise entry and returns the updated session
SessionRepository (via SessionPort) close() returns the session with isOpen set to false
SessionRepository (via SessionPort) addEntry() throws when session is already closed
HistoryService.findHistory excludes sessions older than 30 days when plan is free
HistoryService.findHistory returns all sessions when plan is pro
HistoryService.findHistory includes session at exactly the 30-day cutoff when plan is free

PlateauDetector — flat consecutive sessions trigger warning returns warning for '4 flat' sessions
PlateauDetector — declining reps trigger warning returns warning when reps decline across 3 sessions
PlateauDetector — increasing reps return null returns null when 'monotonically increasing [5,5,6]'
PlateauDetector — increasing reps return null returns null when 'streak broken by final increase [6,7,…'

PlateauDetector — insufficient sessions return null returns null for 'only 2 sessions'
PlateauDetector — sessions sorted internally by loggedAt detects plateau when sessions passed in reverse order (oldest first after sort)
PlateauDetector — sessions sorted internally by loggedAt returns null for improving trend when sessions passed in reverse order
ProgressionRepository (via ProgressionPort) returns current UserProgression when the user has a progression record
ProgressionRepository (via ProgressionPort) returns null when the user has no progression record for the track
ProgressionRepository (via ProgressionPort) returns progression events ordered newest-first for a user with history
ProgressionRepository (via ProgressionPort) returns empty array when the user has no advancement history
ExerciseRepository (via ExercisePort) returns exercises whose name contains the search query
ExerciseRepository (via ExercisePort) returns empty array when query matches no exercises
ExerciseRepository (via ExercisePort) normalizes 'pike pushup' alias to 'pike push-up' before querying Supabase
ExerciseRepository (via ExercisePort) normalizes 'PPP progression' alias to 'pike push-up' before querying Supabase
ReadinessEngine.calculate() calls the Edge Function with userId and exerciseId and returns the parsed signal
ReadinessEngine.calculate() returns null when Edge Function responds with null (no sessions for exercise)
ReadinessEngine.calculate() throws a descriptive error when Edge Function returns an error
